paper_title: Experimental Orchard Study in Casablanca Valley

methods.description: Process for collecting plant species in a vineyard orchard to study their characteristics and pollen production.

steps.description: The study was carried out with organic management in a common vineyard orchard located in the Casablanca Valley (33º20' S; 71º20' W), Valparaíso Region, Chile.
steps.searchable_summary: set up study in organic vineyard orchard in Casablanca Valley

steps.description: Flowers or inflorescences in the pre-anthesis stage of V. vinifera cvs. Chardonnay and Merlot, along with other different associated plant species, were randomly collected between October 2004 and December 2005.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flowers and inflorescences in pre-anthesis stage
inputs: V. vinifera cvs. Chardonnay and Merlot

steps.description: Twelve dominant species were detected in the orchard.
steps.searchable_summary: identify dominant plant species in orchard

steps.description: Recollected weeds belonging to the Asteraceae family gave a very little amount of pollen, except for A. cotula, reason why they were not considered in this assay.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate pollen production of Asteraceae family weeds
